meaning: n.鍐烽叿鏃犳儏,椤藉浐,鎵ф嫍
examples: He shook his head in bewilderment at the obduracy of this man.
For some, including Russia and China thus far, the only answer to Iran's obduracy is more talks.
Yet Mr Scargill-whose obduracy and militancy led his miners to crushing defeat-also haunts Mexborough.
This kind of obduracy isn't new, but it does seem especially egregious among boomers.
The Democrats' top brass blame the party's failings on Republican obduracy and trickery.
